$comment: FC3 c1 dataModel WIDTH differential — the LLP64 (windows) HALF of the datamodel_long_width pair, now a RUNTIME truncation witness (D-CSUBSET-INT-SAME-SIGN-NARROW made implicit narrowing C-conformant, so the old compile-reject verdict became an admit + truncate). Under the pe64 format's declared LLP64, `long` is I32: the I64 long-long-laddered literal 2147483648l narrows to I32 -> 0x80000000 = a NEGATIVE long, so `r > 0` is false -> exit 7. The sibling datamodel_long_width (LP64, long=I64 holds it) exits 42. Host-independent: the dataModel comes from the TARGET format schema. Red-on-disable: stop reading pe64's dataModel (or flip it to LP64) and `long` is I64 -> the literal fits -> r > 0 -> exit 42 (manifest then fails: got 42, expected 7). The release arm runs the shipped optimizing pipeline over the narrowing Trunc + the signed compare.
